name: Chickpea Cassoulet with Tomatoes and Chard
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons chopped fresh thyme (or 1 teaspoon dried thyme)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cook pasta al dente, then drain.
3. In a large skillet, sauté onion, garlic, red pepper flakes, thyme, paprika, and saffron (if using) over medium-high heat until onions are soft, about 8 minutes.
4. Add chickpeas, the liquid from the can of chickpeas, and chopped tomatoes. Season with black pepper.
5. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 15 minutes. If the pan becomes dry, add a little water to keep it moist.
6. Steam chard leaves until bright green and tender. Remove from heat, chop coarsely, and season with additional black pepper and salt (if desired).
7. In a large baking dish, combine pasta, chickpea-tomato mixture, chopped chard, and grated soy cheese. Toss to mix well.
8. Cover the dish with foil and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until warmed through.
9. Serve with additional grated soy cheese if desired. Makes 4 to 6 servings. If fresh chard is unavailable, use frozen spinach instead. Kale would also work well as a substitute. This dish can be prepared ahead of time and reheated in the oven for 20 minutes.
